Oh sure, the 'trading algorithms just stopped working' story. Because apparently every single high-frequency trading system on Earth decided to take a coffee break at exactly the same moment, causing a perfectly synchronized 40% market crash.

Emergency services couldn't handle basic communications after GPS failed, which is totally normal for organizations that existed long before satellites were even invented. I guess radios, landlines, and good old-fashioned maps just evaporated into thin air.

Supply chains collapsed because companies forgot how people moved goods for literally thousands of years before computer logistics. Apparently the ancient art of 'calling someone on the phone' or 'writing things down on paper' became completely impossible overnight.

Digital natives experienced a mental health crisis after 24 whole hours without their phones, as if humans had never survived periods of disconnection before. The New England Journal of Medicine apparently thinks our species is so fragile we can't handle a long weekend without Instagram.

Libraries saw a 3000% visitor surge because people suddenly remembered books exist, which is either the most beautiful coincidence in human history or maybe someone wanted us all to rediscover physical media. Funny how analog information sources became trendy right when digital ones went dark.

Local newspapers magically had the resources to hire emergency staff during a global economic collapse, almost like someone knew this was coming and prepared accordingly. Nothing suspicious about media outlets being ready to fill the exact information void that appeared.

Martinez charged FORTY-SEVEN DOLLARS for a burrito because people only had cash, and you know what, I'M DONE. This is where I draw the line! A taco truck price-gouging during a crisis is the real conspiracy here, not some global digital blackout!
